TestCase z17Change(TestCase t1) {
    TestCase follow_case = t1;
    // --- Memory handling code generated based on the rule ---
    if (t1.n > 0 && t1.a != NULL) {
        int* follow_case_a = (int*)malloc(t1.n * sizeof(int));
        memcpy(follow_case_a, t1.a, t1.n * sizeof(int));
        follow_case.a = follow_case_a;
    } else {
        follow_case.a = NULL;
    }
    // ------------------------------------

    // Defensive code generated based on rule (a) to prevent errors when n < 1
    if (follow_case.n <= 0 || follow_case.a == NULL) {
        // No elements to process or array is NULL, return replica directly
        return follow_case;
    }

    // Specific operation code generated based on the rule
    int random_index = 0;
    // Select random index in safe manner
    // To avoid any dependency on external random generators,
    // use a simple pseudo-random method (for demonstration).
    // Since C++ "rand()" may not be allowed, use modulus of pointer address.
    // But here simply use first element as a fallback to ensure deterministic.
    random_index = (unsigned long)follow_case.a[0] % follow_case.n;

    // Square the element at the random index, protect against overflow is not mandatory here
    int val = follow_case.a[random_index];
    // No domain restrictions on squaring int
    follow_case.a[random_index] = val * val;

    return follow_case;
}
